name: MURAL Visual Collaboration API
what_it_does: MURAL visual collaboration REST API for remote teams and design thinking facilitators to create, manage, and integrate digital whiteboards with sticky notes, frameworks, templates, and real-time collaboration features. Enables AI agents to manage mural workspace and room creation for collaboration environment automation, handle sticky note and shape creation for visual content generation automation, access template and framework instantiation for structured workshop automation, retrieve member and guest access management for collaboration provisioning automation, manage content export and snapshot for collaboration artifact automation, handle mural duplication and template creation for reusable workshop automation, access facilitation timer and voting management for structured session automation, retrieve mural activity and participation tracking for engagement analytics automation, manage workspace member roles and permissions for governance automation, and integrate MURAL with Jira, Microsoft Teams, Zoom, and enterprise tools for end-to-end visual collaboration workflow.
use_cases: Running design thinking workshops from facilitation automation agents
Creating collaborative workspaces from meeting preparation agents
Managing visual retrospectives from Agile ceremony automation agents
Generating mural artifacts from workshop documentation agents
not_for: Diagramming and technical architecture (use Lucidchart or Miro for technical diagrams)
Presentation creation (use Pitch, Canva, or PowerPoint)
Project management with Gantt charts (use Asana, Monday, or Linear)
best_when: A remote team or design thinking facilitator wanting AI agents to automate workshop setup, sticky note generation, template instantiation, and collaboration artifact management through MURAL's visual collaboration platform.
avoid_when: API BETA STATUS AND STABILITY: MURAL API is in beta with ongoing changes; automated workflows built on MURAL API may encounter breaking changes without advance notice; automated production workflows depending on MURAL API stability require monitoring for API version changes and deprecation notices. REAL-TIME COLLABORATION EVENTS NOT AVAILABLE VIA API: MURAL's real-time sticky note and drawing collaboration is WebSocket-based and not accessible via REST API; automated content monitoring cannot subscribe to live mural update events; automated mural state capture must use snapshot or export endpoints rather than real-time streaming. CONTENT SCALE LIMITS FOR AUTOMATED GENERATION: MURAL murals have practical limits on sticky note and element count for performance; automated content generation creating 1000+ sticky notes on a single mural creates rendering performance degradation; automated bulk content generation should distribute content across multiple murals or rooms.

auth.notes: MURAL uses OAuth 2.0 for API access. REST API with JSON. San Francisco, California HQ. Founded 2011 by Mariano Suarez-Battan and Agustin Soler. Backed by Radian Capital, Gradient Ventures, Tiger Global ($118M raised). Products: Digital whiteboard, facilitation tools, templates, guest access, integrations. Integrations: Jira, Microsoft Teams, Zoom, Slack, Google Workspace. GDPR. SOC2. Serves enterprise design and product teams. Competes with Miro, FigJam, and Lucidchart for digital whiteboard.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free plan (3 murals, unlimited members); limited storage and features
pricing.notes: San Francisco CA. VC-backed. Free tier (3 murals). Per-member subscription. Annual discount.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; mural and content queries throttled; webhooks for mural, member, and content events

known_agent_gotchas: API BETA STATUS — BREAKING CHANGES POSSIBLE: MURAL REST API is in beta; automated integrations must monitor MURAL developer changelog for breaking changes; automated production workflows should implement API version pinning and circuit breakers for MURAL API changes

MURAL vs ROOM vs WORKSPACE HIERARCHY: MURAL organizes content in Workspaces → Rooms → Murals; automated content creation must specify correct hierarchy; automated mural creation without room context creates mural in default room rather than intended collaboration space

STICKY NOTE CONTENT FIELD ENCODING: MURAL sticky note content supports HTML-encoded text; automated sticky note creation with plain text must handle HTML encoding; automated unencoded special characters (&, <, >) in sticky note content creates rendering issues in MURAL interface

TEMPLATE INSTANTIATION vs MURAL DUPLICATION: MURAL supports both template instantiation (creates from template) and mural duplication (copies existing mural); automated workshop setup using duplication copies participant-specific content; automated workshop setup should use template instantiation for clean workshop structure

GUEST ACCESS LINK EXPIRY FOR EXTERNAL PARTICIPANTS: MURAL guest links can be configured with expiry; automated external stakeholder collaboration using guest links must track and refresh link expiry; automated distribution of expired guest links creates access denied for external workshop participants
agent_readiness.security_notes: Visual collaboration. GDPR, SOC2. OAuth2. US/EU. Whiteboard content and collaboration data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Visual collaboration. GDPR, SOC2. OAuth2. US/EU. Whiteboard and collaboration data.
